questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics

questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics
questions.stem: A force of $20\,\text{kN}$ acts perpendicular to a lever at a distance of $3\,\text{m}$ from the pivot. The moment about the pivot is _____ kN·m.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $M = F\times d$ for a perpendicular force.

**Working.**
$$M = 20\times 3 = 60.$$

**Answer.** $60\,\text{kN·m}$

questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics
questions.stem: A force of $15\,\text{kN}$ acts perpendicular to a lever at a distance of $4\,\text{m}$ from the pivot. The moment about the pivot is _____ kN·m.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $M = F\times d$ for a perpendicular force.

**Working.**
$$M = 15\times 4 = 60.$$

**Answer.** $60\,\text{kN·m}$

questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics
questions.stem: A force of $25\,\text{kN}$ acts perpendicular to a lever at a distance of $2\,\text{m}$ from the pivot. The moment about the pivot is _____ kN·m.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $M = F\times d$ for a perpendicular force.

**Working.**
$$M = 25\times 2 = 50.$$

**Answer.** $50\,\text{kN·m}$

questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics
questions.stem: The centroid of a triangular section of height $300\,\text{mm}$ lies at a distance of _____ mm from its base.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Centroid of a triangle is at $h/3$ from the base.

**Working.**
$$\bar y = \dfrac{300}{3} = 100.$$

**Answer.** $100\,\text{mm}$

questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics
questions.stem: The centroid of a triangular section of height $600\,\text{mm}$ lies at a distance of _____ mm from its base.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Centroid of a triangle is at $h/3$ from the base.

**Working.**
$$\bar y = \dfrac{600}{3} = 200.$$

**Answer.** $200\,\text{mm}$

questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics
questions.stem: The centroid of a triangular section of height $450\,\text{mm}$ lies at a distance of _____ mm from its base.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Centroid of a triangle is at $h/3$ from the base.

**Working.**
$$\bar y = \dfrac{450}{3} = 150.$$

**Answer.** $150\,\text{mm}$

questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics
questions.stem: A member of a perfect pin-jointed truss carrying no load (a zero-force member) is identified primarily to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Zero-force members carry no axial force under the given loading and are removed to simplify analysis.

**Working.**
They simplify analysis.

**Answer.** Simplify analysis

questions.subject: Engineering Mechanics
questions.stem: A simply supported beam (one hinge, one roller) is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 3 reaction components, 3 equilibrium equations → determinate.

**Working.**
Determinate.

**Answer.** Statically determinate
